creator: @soyenriquerocha
executive_summary: For a moderate risk profile, we recommend a defensively-tilted portfolio that capitalizes on the Iran war's market dislocations. Silver (XAG) is the top overall pick — 6th year of structural supply deficit with solar/EV/AI demand, 30% below ATH. Broadcom (AVGO) leads equities with 106% AI chip revenue growth and blowout guidance. Chainlink (LINK) is the top crypto pick at 70% below highs with 90% oracle market dominance. The AUD/USD long captures the RBA's hawkish pivot. Coffee Arabica is a rare sell opportunity on Brazil's record harvest. Dynamic asset discovery reveals opportunities the market is missing — from Bittensor's AI breakthrough to Devon Energy's oil hedge to Turkey's lira crisis.

macro_environment.summary: The macro environment is dominated by the Iran war (Operation Epic Fury, week 3), which has pushed oil above $98/barrel, gold past $5,000/oz, and triggered the largest IEA reserve release in history (400M barrels). Seven central bank decisions are scheduled March 16-19 (RBA, BoC, FOMC, BoJ, ECB, BoE, SNB) — creating a historic 'super-week' for forex. Recession probability at 30-35% as stagflation concerns mount from energy-driven inflation.
key_factors: Iran war week 3 — Strait of Hormuz blockade disrupting 20% of global oil/LNG supply
7 central bank decisions March 16-19 — historic 'super-week' including FOMC, BoJ, ECB
Fed expected to cut 25bps at March 26 meeting (60% probability) despite inflation
IEA released record 400M barrels from emergency reserves — largest ever
Qatar Ras Laffan LNG facility shut after Iranian drone attack — European gas +46%
Fear & Greed Index recovered from 8 to 45 — fastest recovery in over a year
COT data shows USD shorts at 5-year extreme — short-squeeze risk

cross_sector_insights.insight: Silver and AI tokens (TAO, RENDER) both surging 30%+ weekly — decentralized infrastructure demand across physical and digital
cross_sector_insights.implication: The supply deficit narrative is playing out simultaneously in silver (solar/EV demand) and decentralized compute (AI demand). Both are structural, not speculative. Double exposure through XAG + LINK/TAO.

cross_sector_insights.insight: Oil at $98 WTI while Bitcoin gained 4% since Iran strikes began — crypto proving safe-haven thesis
cross_sector_insights.implication: BTC is decoupling from risk-off moves for the first time in a major conflict. Institutional adoption is changing crypto's correlation structure. Moderate crypto allocation warranted even in risk-off environments.

cross_sector_insights.insight: Coffee down 20% while corn range-bound — agricultural commodities diverging from energy/metals
cross_sector_insights.implication: Not all commodities benefit from the Iran crisis. Brazil's record coffee harvest creates a rare short opportunity in an otherwise bullish commodity environment. Coffee sell is the highest-conviction bearish call.

cross_sector_insights.insight: 7 central bank decisions in 4 days (March 16-19) while USD shorts at 5-year extreme
cross_sector_insights.implication: Historic volatility catalyst. If even 2-3 banks surprise hawkish, USD short-squeeze could cause rapid moves across all forex pairs. AUD/USD long benefits from RBA rate hike but hedge with tight stops.

cross_sector_insights.insight: AVGO +106% AI chip revenue while Devon Energy +22% YTD — the new market leadership rotation
cross_sector_insights.implication: The old FAANG leadership is fragmenting. AI infrastructure (AVGO, NVDA) and energy (DVN) are the new leaders. Amazon is the bridge — both AI cloud and consumer. Portfolio should reflect this rotation.

crypto.sector_summary: The crypto market is recovering from extreme fear (Fear & Greed hit 8 in mid-February) amid the US-Iran war backdrop, with Bitcoin proving its institutional safe-haven thesis by gaining 4% since strikes began. AI tokens (TAO, RENDER, FET) are leading the recovery with 30%+ weekly gains driven by the agentic AI narrative and Nvidia's bullish commentary. Most major assets remain 40-70% below their 2025 highs, creating compelling entry points for long-term investors as institutional adoption accelerates (NYSE blockchain trading, CCIP banking adoption, spot ETF infrastructure).
crypto.top_pick_reasoning: Chainlink at $9.36 offers the best risk-adjusted opportunity in crypto right now. It owns 90% of the DeFi oracle market, its CCIP protocol has become the banking standard for cross-chain interoperability, and it's pivoting into AI — placing it at the intersection of RWA tokenization, institutional DeFi, and AI infrastructure. At 70% below its 52-week high with proven institutional adoption and revenue-generating utility, LINK is a blue-chip token priced like a speculative altcoin. While TAO and RENDER offer higher short-term momentum, LINK's fundamental moat and institutional traction make it the highest-conviction pick for risk-adjusted returns over the next 3-6 months.

stocks.sector_summary: U.S. equities are under significant pressure in mid-March 2026 as the Iran conflict drives oil prices above $110/bbl and triggers a risk-off rotation out of tech into energy and defense. The S&P 500 is down 1.54% YTD at its lowest close of the year, while the NASDAQ has fallen 3.8% YTD as megacap tech bears the brunt. However, the pullback is creating compelling entry points in quality AI/cloud names like Broadcom and Amazon, while energy stocks like Devon Energy are direct beneficiaries of the geopolitical backdrop.
stocks.top_pick_reasoning: Broadcom offers the best risk/reward in the current market. It just delivered a beat-and-raise quarter with AI chip revenue growing 106% YoY, CEO visibility to $100B+ AI revenue by 2027, and Q2 guidance crushing estimates by $1.4B. Despite these accelerating fundamentals, the stock is 22% below its 52-week high due to macro/geopolitical fears — not business deterioration. BofA's top semiconductor pick with multiple analyst upgrades. The combination of verified earnings acceleration, reasonable valuation relative to growth, and a macro-driven discount makes AVGO the most compelling opportunity across all names surveyed.

The carry trade offers offset but geopolitical proximity to Iran conflict and potential for accelerated depreciation create two-way risk. Goldman's 48 target implies 8.6% upside but timing is uncertain. Better to wait for a pullback to 42-43 for fresh USD/TRY longs.

assets.name: Australian Dollar / US Dollar
assets.sentiment: mixed — RBA hawkish pivot (potential March 17 hike) vs. dollar safe-haven bid and China slowdown fears
assets.social_buzz: high — RBA March 17 decision is first of super-week, 75% chance of rate hike priced in
key_news: RBA March 17 meeting 'live' — 75% chance of rate hike priced in, up from 30% earlier this week
RBA Deputy Governor flagged rising oil prices could push inflation higher, triggering hawkish repricing
Australian headline inflation at 3.8%, core at 3.4% — well above 2-3% target band
AUD/USD at 3-year highs near 0.71, up ~10% from 2025 average of 0.64
Asset managers flipped to net-long AUD for first time since October — large specs most bullish since Oct 2017
China growth concerns and iron ore price weakness are AUD headwinds
social_highlights: RBA rate hike would make Australia one of very few DM central banks tightening — bullish AUD signal
0.70 support level being closely watched — a hold above supports further upside to 0.72-0.75
assets.reasoning: AUD/USD is the most compelling long trade in G10 FX right now. The RBA is pivoting hawkish while almost every other major central bank is easing or holding — this policy divergence is powerful. A rate hike on March 17 would reinforce AUD strength. COT positioning confirms institutional backing. The key risk is a broader risk-off event intensifying the dollar bid, but the carry and rate differential story favors AUD on a multi-week basis. Buy dips near 0.70.

currencies.sector_summary: The forex market in mid-March 2026 is dominated by two mega-themes: the Iran war driving a massive oil shock and safe-haven dollar rally, and a historic 'super-week' of 7 central bank decisions between March 16-19. The dollar has surged 2.5% since the conflict began, benefiting from its unique position as both safe haven and net oil exporter. Emerging market currencies are suffering their worst month since late 2024, with central banks in Indonesia, Turkey, and India forced to intervene. The key question for next week: does the FOMC signal delayed cuts (dollar bullish) or does the BoJ hike to 1% (yen bullish).
currencies.top_pick_reasoning: AUD/USD offers the best risk-adjusted trade: the RBA is one of the only G10 central banks pivoting hawkish with a 75% chance of hiking on March 17, while COT positioning confirms institutional conviction (most bullish since 2017). At 0.7061, the pair has strong support at 0.70 with upside targets of 0.72-0.75. The rate hike catalyst is imminent and well-defined. While the dollar is strong on safe-haven flows, AUD benefits from Australia's commodity exposure and terms-of-trade improvement from higher energy prices. This is a high-conviction, event-driven buy with clear risk management at the 0.70 level.
